intent: A project management platform designed to automate task prioritization using an AI-driven engine that analyzes team bandwidth, skill sets, and deadline urgency to generate optimized daily workflows. Its unique value proposition is the reduction of 'administrative drag' by automatically updating project timelines based on real-time task progress and predicting potential bottlenecks before they impact delivery dates.

entities.name: ResourceCapacityModel
entities.description: A quantitative profile of team members capturing bandwidth availability, domain-specific skill proficiency, and historical velocity metrics.

entities.name: TaskDependencyGraph
entities.description: A directed acyclic graph representing the relational structure of tasks, including hard constraints, prerequisite sequences, and critical path identification.

entities.name: PredictiveWorkflowEngine
entities.description: An AI inference service that ingest real-time telemetry to output optimized task sequences and probability scores for milestone delivery.

entities.name: TelemetryEventStream
entities.description: A high-throughput event bus capturing granular progress updates, such as Git commits, Slack status changes, and calendar block events.

entities.name: BottleneckHeuristicService
entities.description: An analytical monitor that compares current task burn-down rates against baseline velocity to detect emergent project risks.

entities.name: AccessControlContext
entities.description: A granular permission container enforcing RBAC policies across departments to ensure secure, scoped data visibility.

entities.name: ExternalIntegrationAdapter
entities.description: A modular gateway facilitating bi-directional synchronization between the platform and third-party tools like Slack, Teams, and calendar providers.

architecture.description: The system employs an event-driven architecture where the ExternalIntegrationAdapter normalizes incoming telemetry from communication tools and calendar services into the TelemetryEventStream. This stream updates the ResourceCapacityModel and TaskDependencyGraph in near real-time. The PredictiveWorkflowEngine subscribes to these updates, utilizing historical task completion rates to recalculate optimized daily schedules. The BottleneckHeuristicService continuously runs against the TaskDependencyGraph to identify risk vectors, triggering proactive alerts. AccessControlContext validates all state changes against defined organizational roles. Data is persisted in a distributed cloud database, ensuring eventual consistency across global regions while maintaining low-latency read access for collaborative dashboarding.

endpoints.summary: Retrieve the current task dependency graph structure
Ingest normalized telemetry events from integrations
Retrieve latest optimized task sequences and milestone probabilities
Retrieve current bottleneck risk vectors and burn-down analytics
Configure a new external integration gateway
List all organizational roles and permission mappings

rules.description: Ensure telemetry integrity by validating schema conformance before ingestion into TelemetryEventStream
rules.logic: If incoming event payload lacks required metadata fields (source_id, timestamp, event_type), discard and log as a malformed event.

rules.description: Prevent circular dependency injection into TaskDependencyGraph
rules.logic: Validate that any new task relation does not create a cycle using Kahn's algorithm or equivalent DAG traversal before persisting.

rules.description: Enforce RBAC scoped access for state transition requests
rules.logic: Verify that the AccessControlContext grants permission for the requester's role to modify the specific entity status (e.g., Task completion or Capacity change).

rules.description: Trigger recalibration of project schedule upon significant velocity deviation
rules.logic: If the difference between current burn-down rate and historical velocity baseline exceeds 20%, invoke the PredictiveWorkflowEngine for schedule re-optimization.

rules.description: Synchronize capacity availability with calendar event changes
rules.logic: If a user's calendar event status changes to 'Busy', decrement the ResourceCapacityModel availability score proportionally for the duration of the event.

rules.description: Ensure eventual consistency for distributed read replicas
rules.logic: Maintain a version vector on each ResourceCapacityModel object; reject updates that arrive with an out-of-order sequence number.
